title: arunner harness result record
description: Terminal record written by `tick.py` to results/result-NNNNN.json when a run reaches a terminal state. Sourced from the worker's terminal heartbeat sentinel (completed/failed) or synthesized by the harness (auth_or_launch_failed — no worker terminal was ever emitted).

terminal_status.description: Upper-cased terminal status. auth_or_launch_failed runs record FAILED with synthesized=true.

result_file.description: Path of the worker's result file (quality/SUMMARY.md or equivalent), from the terminal heartbeat. null when synthesized.

summary.description: One-line outcome from the terminal heartbeat, or the synthesis reason.

reaped_ts.description: ISO8601 UTC time the harness reaped this run.

synthesized.description: true when the harness wrote this record WITHOUT a worker terminal heartbeat (launch failure / abandon).

anomaly.description: Set when a guard fired during reap (e.g. claimed_job_file_absent_at_reap) — the transition completed on the heartbeat's authority but the on-disk claimed file was unexpectedly absent.

claimed.description: Snapshot of the claimed job file at reap (the wrapped plan entry), when it was present.

input_tokens.description: FR-65 (optional): input token count for this run/step, read by the engine from the worker's terminal-or-latest heartbeat `data.usage.input_tokens` at reap (the engine reads EXACTLY data.usage and nothing else in the opaque data object). A TOP-LEVEL property — a sibling of summary/synthesized, NOT nested under claimed. null/absent when the worker reported no usage (rendered '—', never a fabricated 0/estimate, NFR-12). Reporting-only — never interpreted for control flow (FR-18/FR-5 {done,stop} untouched).

output_tokens.description: FR-65 (optional): output token count, read from `data.usage.output_tokens` at reap. Top-level sibling of input_tokens (not under claimed); null/absent → '—'. Summed across runs/steps for the entry/run TOKENS roll-up; reporting-only, never control-flow.
